Ref: {{ref}}
ESOP Term Sheet
Proposed Terms — 100% ESOP Transaction
Company
{{companyName}}
Seller
{{sellerName}}
Trustee
{{trusteeName}}
Date
{{generatedDate}}
§ 01Parties
{{sellerName}} (the “Seller”)
{{companyName}} (the “Company”)
{{trusteeName}} (the “Trustee”)
Note: The Trustee acts as an independent fiduciary for the ESOP and its participants. The Trustee is not an agent of the Seller or the Company.
§ 02Transaction Structure
100% ESOP acquisition via a newly created employee stock ownership plan
New ESOP (not a leveraged expansion of an existing plan)
The ESOP trust, acting through the Trustee
C-Corporation (required for §1042 election)
§ 03Purchase Price & Valuation
{{purchasePrice}}
{{valuationBasis}}
Fair market value per IRC §401(a)(28)(C) and DOL Regulation §2550.408c-2
Adequacy requirement: The Trustee must independently determine that the purchase price does not exceed fair market value. The Trustee's valuation firm will issue a formal appraisal and FMV adequacy letter prior to closing.
§ 04Payment Terms
{{cashAtClosing}}
{{sellerNotePrincipal}}
{{sellerNoteRate}}
{{sellerNoteTerm}}
Seller note terms: The seller note is subordinated to senior debt. Interest accrues from closing; principal payments begin upon release from subordination. The note is secured by the assets of the Company and guaranteed by the ESOP trust to the extent of its assets.
§ 05Key Conditions Precedent

Closing is conditioned upon satisfaction of each of the following:

1.Financing. SBA 7(a) loan commitment or equivalent senior financing approved and documented.
2.Appraisal. Independent valuation confirms purchase price at or below fair market value; Trustee issues FMV adequacy letter.
3.ERISA Compliance. ERISA counsel confirms the transaction complies with ERISA §406 and applicable prohibited transaction exemptions.
4.QofE. Quality of Earnings report completed; normalized EBITDA within acceptable range of projections.
5.Plan Documents. ESOP plan document, trust agreement, and related documents executed.
6.Legal Opinions. Tax opinion confirming §1042 eligibility; corporate opinion confirming authority and enforceability.
7.Due Diligence. Satisfactory completion of financial, legal, and operational due diligence by the Trustee and lender.
§ 06Timeline & Milestones
Upon signing by all parties
Upon term sheet execution
{{targetClosing}}
Process: Following term sheet execution, Forhemit will coordinate the transaction team through a structured process including Gate reviews at key milestones. The target closing date assumes no material delays in financing, appraisal, or legal review.
§ 07Exclusivity
{{exclusivityPeriod}}

During the Exclusivity Period, the Seller agrees not to solicit, entertain, or negotiate any offer for the sale of the Company or its assets from any third party. The Seller shall promptly notify the Trustee of any unsolicited offer received during this period.

Scope: Exclusivity applies to the sale of the Company's equity, assets, or any combination thereof. It does not restrict the Company's ordinary business operations.
§ 08Confidentiality

All parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality regarding the terms of this term sheet and all information exchanged in connection with the proposed transaction. This obligation survives termination of the term sheet for a period of two (2) years.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, each party may disclose confidential information to its professional advisors (legal, tax, financial) who are bound by professional confidentiality obligations, and as required by law or regulation.

§ 09Non-Binding Nature
IMPORTANT: This term sheet is non-binding and is intended solely as a basis for further negotiation. No party shall have any binding obligation under this term sheet except for the Confidentiality and Exclusivity provisions (§07 and §08), which shall be binding upon execution. A binding agreement will be set forth in a definitive purchase agreement and related transaction documents.
